title: dryopteris brackenridgei
intro: Dryopteris brackenridgei, also known as Brackenridge's wood fern, is a species of fern in the Dryopteris genus, native to the Hawaiian Islands. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Dryopteris brackenridgei include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a valuable component in traditional Hawaiian medicine. Dryopteris brackenridgei is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its extracts are also used in pharmaceutical applications.
